title: THETA Control Room — Build Hook Delta

changedProjects.lastKnownStatus: Build hook reported latest project state.
changedProjects.nextAction: Run CATHODE smoke tests and Deep QA ticket folders.
changedProjects.tomorrowFirstAction: Run CATHODE smoke tests and Deep QA ticket folders.

tickets.question: Does the CATHODE source zip build from a clean folder without requiring any older CATHODE zip or manual resource copy?
tickets.acceptance: Build starts from the current source zip alone, finds Montserrat and JetBrains Mono in Resources, and does not attempt to unzip v16.38 or any older package.
tickets.testMaterial: Clean ~/Documents/_PLUGINS/zJUCE_SOURCES/CATHODE/cathode_src_v16.74 folder, no old cathode_src folders required.

tickets.question: Does CATHODE appear correctly in Ableton after install/rescan?
tickets.acceptance: CATHODE v16.74 appears as an audio effect under THETA Audio Research with no stale duplicate taking precedence.
tickets.testMaterial: Ableton Live VST3/AU rescan, insert on audio track.

tickets.question: Does the CATHODE installer experience show the intended CATHODE artwork in the DMG and/or PKG welcome path?
tickets.acceptance: Mounted DMG shows the CATHODE background artwork or a clean branded installer view; PKG welcome is acceptable if macOS displays it.
tickets.testMaterial: Open mounted CATHODE DMG and PKG on tester Mac; capture screenshots.

tickets.question: Does the Control Room hook report build/install/package status through /api/delta using local env credentials?
tickets.acceptance: Terminal prints successful CATHODE report, Control Room updates version/artifact/status, and missing token or curl failure never fails the build.
tickets.testMaterial: Run Scripts/control_room_report.sh manual-hook-test and normal package build with ~/.theta_control_room_env configured.

tickets.question: Does first open pass clean dry audio with all modules off?
tickets.acceptance: No gain jump, static, crush, drift, crackle, or coloration until modules are enabled or a preset is loaded.
tickets.testMaterial: Full-range music loop, spoken vocal, sustained sine/pad.

tickets.question: Can LOCK, HEAD, RECEIVER, PHOSPHOR, INTERFERENCE, and CRUSH toggle during playback without clicks/pops?
tickets.acceptance: All module engage/disengage actions are smoothed and do not reset DSP state audibly.
tickets.testMaterial: Sustained pad, low sine, transient drum loop, spoken vocal.

tickets.question: Does BYPASS crossfade smoothly and resume the running DSP state?
tickets.acceptance: No click/dropout; returning from bypass does not restart modulation or wipe internal state.
tickets.testMaterial: Active INTERFERENCE + CRUSH processing while toggling BYPASS repeatedly.

tickets.question: Does REC/STOP REC capture processed stereo output correctly?
tickets.acceptance: REC prompts for a WAV path, STOP REC ends cleanly, file plays back with processed stereo output and no truncation.
tickets.testMaterial: Processed stereo loop with several active modules.

tickets.question: Does CATHODE still feel like a damaged receiver/transmission instrument rather than generic lo-fi?
tickets.acceptance: The combined modules create transmission stress, drift, dropout, takeover, glow, and converter damage with coherent CATHODE identity.
tickets.testMaterial: Clean vocal, drums, full mix, synth pad, sparse piano/guitar loop.

tickets.question: Does LOCK sound like unstable continuity/sync stress rather than generic tremolo/glitch?
tickets.acceptance: WARP, SLIP, RECOVER/SPEED, and MIX create musical relock/timing stress without unintended destruction.
tickets.testMaterial: Steady drum groove, sustained organ/pad, dialogue stem.

tickets.question: Does HEAD feel like worn contact and mechanical mistracking rather than simple chorus/LFO pitch movement?
tickets.acceptance: WEAR and DRIFT create physical instability and contact variation without obvious repeating loops.
tickets.testMaterial: Sustained piano, vocal, guitar chord, wide pad.

tickets.question: Does RECEIVER sound like AGC breathing and continuity loss rather than generic pumping?
tickets.acceptance: PUMP/SPEED and DROP/RATE/DEPTH create receiver compensation, dropout, and recovery behavior linked to signal stress.
tickets.testMaterial: Dialogue stem, dynamic music loop, sustained pad.

tickets.question: Does INTERFERENCE feel like rival-station contested broadcast behavior rather than static layered on top?
tickets.acceptance: BURST, TAKEOVER, NOISE, STATIC, MOTION, and MIX create competition, seizure, ducking, and RF conflict with musical control.
tickets.testMaterial: Clean vocal or spoken word with and without a loaded rival file.

tickets.question: Does CRUSH sound like broken converter/digital receiver damage rather than generic bitcrush?
tickets.acceptance: BITS, RATE, ALIAS, JITTER, MIX, FILTER, DITHER, MOD, DRIVE, and FOLD combine into controlled digital destruction with usable range.
tickets.testMaterial: Drum loop, bright synth, vocal, full mix, low-level ambience.

tickets.question: Do CRUSH BITS and RATE show correct units instead of percentages?
tickets.acceptance: BITS shows bit depth; RATE shows Hz/kHz values while moving controls.
tickets.testMaterial: CRUSH main page, full BITS/RATE sweeps.

tickets.question: Does CRUSH ADV modulation avoid zipper crackle during cycles?
tickets.acceptance: Modulating BITS/RATE/BOTH with LFO/ENV produces intentional degradation only, not unwanted cycle crackle.
tickets.testMaterial: Sustained pad, sine, vocal, full mix using CRUSH MOD SRC LFO and ENV.

tickets.question: Did INTERFERENCE static frequency movement stop sounding like an obvious repeating loop?
tickets.acceptance: STATIC DRIFT and STATIC SMOOTH feel non-repeating and RF-like over at least 60 seconds.
tickets.testMaterial: Quiet source and full-range loop with INTERFERENCE STATIC/DRIFT/SMOOTH active.

tickets.question: Do MOTION LFO destinations RATE, LENGTH, FREQ, and TAKEOVER affect the intended INTERFERENCE behavior?
tickets.acceptance: Each destination has a clear audible/visual effect; disabling all destinations removes LFO influence.
tickets.testMaterial: INTERFERENCE MOTION EDIT, one destination active at a time.

tickets.question: Does SHAPE remain a dropdown with SINE, TRIANGLE, SAW, SQUARE, RANDOM, and DRIFT?
tickets.acceptance: SHAPE is not a slider, maps correctly, and recalls through presets/sessions.
tickets.testMaterial: INTERFERENCE MOTION EDIT page and preset/session recall.

tickets.question: Is the UI version visible and correct as v16.74?
tickets.acceptance: The plugin visibly reports v16.74 so the tester can confirm the installed build.
tickets.testMaterial: Open plugin at default 1x in Ableton and standalone.

tickets.question: Is small UI text readable at 100% scale on 1920x1080, 2K, and Retina displays?
tickets.acceptance: Module names, slider labels, dropdown text, button text, version string, MOTION EDIT labels, and values are legible at normal seated distance without squinting or blur.
tickets.testMaterial: Default 1x screenshots on 1920x1080 and any available 2K/Retina display.

tickets.question: Is CRUSH SCOPE text placement restored and not covering the waveform?
tickets.acceptance: CRUSH SCOPE/CONVERTER DAMAGE and telemetry are readable without obstructing the waveform or transfer hint.
tickets.testMaterial: Open CRUSH ADV with CRUSH on/off and capture screenshot.

tickets.question: Do INTERFERENCE and CRUSH module preset dropdowns fit without overlap?
tickets.acceptance: PRESET controls align right and do not collide with titles, SAVE AS/SAVE, LOAD/CLEAR/LFO, or ADV.
tickets.testMaterial: Default 1x, normal and advanced module modes.

tickets.question: Do contextual visual bays route correctly for MOTION EDIT and CRUSH ADV?
tickets.acceptance: MOTION EDIT replaces LOCK+HEAD with MOTION LFO while CRT stays SIGNAL DAMAGE unless CRUSH ADV is open; CRUSH ADV switches CRT to CRUSH SCOPE; both can be open together.
tickets.testMaterial: Open/close MOTION EDIT and CRUSH ADV in different orders.

tickets.question: Does global SAVE overwrite the selected user preset and protect factory/default presets?
tickets.acceptance: User preset changes persist on SAVE; factory/default selections route to SAVE AS or otherwise avoid overwrite.
tickets.testMaterial: Create user preset, edit, SAVE, reload; then test DEFAULT/factory protection.

tickets.question: Do INTERFERENCE and CRUSH module presets load/save only their own module parameters?
tickets.acceptance: INTERFERENCE presets do not alter CRUSH/LOCK/HEAD/RECEIVER/PHOSPHOR; CRUSH presets do not alter INTERFERENCE or other modules.
tickets.testMaterial: Factory and user module presets for both modules.

tickets.question: Does DAW project reopen restore CATHODE state correctly?
tickets.acceptance: Module on/off, parameters, CRUSH ADV, MOTION shape/destinations, module presets, and global preset selection restore as expected.
tickets.testMaterial: Save and reopen Ableton project with active CATHODE settings.

tickets.question: Is CPU stable with SIGNAL DAMAGE, MOTION LFO, and CRUSH SCOPE visuals active?
tickets.acceptance: No runaway CPU, UI lag, or host stutter from visual timers at default 1x or larger size.
tickets.testMaterial: Ableton CPU meter with normal, MOTION, CRUSH ADV, and both visual bays open.

tickets.question: Does the install replace stale CATHODE versions cleanly?
tickets.acceptance: DAW and standalone show v16.74 after install/rescan with no older cached bundle taking precedence.
tickets.testMaterial: Install, rescan, open VST3/AU/standalone.

tickets.question: Does Control Room reporting remain non-fatal under missing token, bad token, or bad endpoint?
tickets.acceptance: Build/install/package still succeeds; script prints Control Room reporting failed; continuing build. on curl failure and exits 0.
tickets.testMaterial: Unset token, invalid token, invalid endpoint, offline network if possible.

tickets.question: What should the next CATHODE build focus on after v16.74 testing?
tickets.acceptance: Tester notes identify one clear next build priority with screenshot/audio/log evidence where applicable.
tickets.testMaterial: All v16.74 test notes, screenshots, audio clips, installer screenshots, Control Room report status.

tickets.question: Did Control Room receive and refresh the CATHODE categorized ticket folders from ticketPacket?
tickets.acceptance: Control Room product detail shows updated sync timestamp and categorized/collapsible CATHODE ticket folders matching ControlRoom/test_tickets.json.
tickets.testMaterial: Run manual-hook-test, inspect Control Room product card and ticket folders.

tickets.question: Do MOTION EDIT SYNC and RETRIG controls behave safely and musically?
tickets.acceptance: SYNC FREE preserves old free-running behavior; SYNC HOST follows host tempo when transport tempo is available; RETRIG BAR resets motion phase at bar starts while falling back safely if the host provides no bar position.
tickets.testMaterial: Ableton session at 90, 120, and 160 BPM with INTERFERENCE MOTION active on RATE/LENGTH/FREQ/TAKEOVER destinations.

tickets.question: Do MOTION SYNC and RETRIG dropdowns expose the full v16.74 option sets?
tickets.acceptance: SYNC dropdown shows FREE, HOST, BAR, BEAT. RETRIG dropdown shows NOTE first, then FREE, BAR, LOAD. FREE remains safe for old sessions unless NOTE is selected.
tickets.testMaterial: Open INTERFERENCE MOTION EDIT, inspect both dropdown menus, then test each option while host transport runs.
